mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and driving positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand, triggering corporate earnings revisions downward and depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth decelerates while consumer credit outstanding simultaneously expands, it indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand is sustained by debt rather than income, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05 across three independent market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a month-over-month decline in housing starts reduces construction activity and consumer wealth effects, leading to lower aggregate demand and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines consistently coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening labor market conditions that support corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as earnings momentum is priced in.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in payroll growth months are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 3.5% year-over-year growth, persistent inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the inflation threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated, the combination of weakening economic activity and tight financial conditions triggers a risk-off repricing, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05 across three independent market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained macro regime, a sustained rise in the unemployment rate indicates structural labor market slack that will eventually force wage growth to moderate, easing input costs and ultimately supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon as margin expectations improve.
failure_condition: Employment growth months consistently produce negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations during the specified recovery regime.
